title: Anthoxanthum novae-zelandiae
intro: Anthoxanthum novae-zelandiae, commonly known as New Zealand sweet grass or New Zealand sweet vernal grass, is a perennial grass native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. New Zealand sweet grass is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into herbal remedies for its soothing and calming effects.
taxonomy: Anthoxanthum novae-zelandiae is a species of grass native to New Zealand. It belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. This species is further classified under the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. The genus Anthoxanthum includes several species of grass, with Anthoxanthum novae-zelandiae being one of the more specific classifications. This classification places Anthoxanthum novae-zelandiae within the broader context of grasses.
